task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI application is launched and the System Control Panel is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is open, displaying the initial System Control Panel where data sources and settings are configured.
In the System Control Panel, the 'SYNTHETIC' (or algorithmic) option is highlighted or selected as the data source.

task_completion.description: The new user profile name 'Subject_Alpha' is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Subject_Alpha' is visible in the user profile creation text field or dialog within the OpenBCI GUI.

task_completion.description: The 'Subject_Alpha' profile is selected as the active profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user profile dropdown or active profile indicator in the System Control Panel displays 'Subject_Alpha'.

task_completion.description: A session is started using the selected profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenBCI GUI workspace is visible (showing data visualization widgets like Time Series, FFT, etc.), indicating that the 'START SESSION' button was clicked and the session is running.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenBCI GUI interface being clicked and typed into, rather than terminal commands creating directories or files directly.

integrity.description: The profile name was entered into the GUI's profile creation field.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Subject_Alpha' appears in the OpenBCI GUI's specific text input field for user profiles, not in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The session started is a genuine OpenBCI workspace.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final state shows the standard OpenBCI GUI data visualization interface, confirming the application transitioned from the System Control Panel to the active session.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a user profile named 'Subject_Alpha' and starting a Synthetic session in OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2. The GUI runs on a Linux desktop environment. The user profile must be created within the System Control Panel of the GUI. Synthetic data is a built-in algorithmic data source in OpenBCI that does not require real hardware.
